votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification, accurately counting standard vowels and checking if the last character is 'y' or 'Y'.
The Python code correctly implements the vowel counting logic and the special handling for the letter 'y' at the end of the string as defined in the Coq specification.
The Python code accurately translates the Coq definitions, correctly counting standard vowels and checking if the last character is 'y'.

The Python code correctly implements all the Coq definitions: is_standard_vowel checks the same 10 vowel characters, count_standard_vowels sums 1 for each vowel in the string, is_y checks for 'y' or 'Y', last_char_is_y checks if the last character is y (using s[-1] which is equivalent to get (length s - 1) s for non-empty strings), and vowels_count_spec returns the same boolean judgment as the Coq Prop would evaluate to for concrete inputs.

The Python code correctly implements all the Coq definitions: is_standard_vowel checks the same 10 vowel characters, count_standard_vowels sums 1 for each vowel in the string, is_y checks for 'y' or 'Y', last_char_is_y checks if the last character is y (using s[-1] which is equivalent to get (length s - 1) s for non-empty strings), and vowels_count_spec returns whether cnt equals the sum of standard vowels plus 1 if the last char is y. The Python returns a bool for the spec while Coq uses Prop, but for concrete inputs they produce the same judgment.

The Python code correctly implements all the Coq definitions: is_standard_vowel checks the same 10 vowel characters, count_standard_vowels sums 1 for each vowel in the string, is_y checks for 'y' or 'Y', last_char_is_y checks if the last character is y (using s[-1] which is equivalent to get (length s - 1) s for non-empty strings), and vowels_count_spec returns the same boolean judgment as the Coq Prop would evaluate to for concrete inputs.
Python mirrors Coq: same vowel set, per-character counting, last-character y check (including empty-string case), and same final equality.
Python mirrors Coq: same vowel set, counts per-character, last-char check for y on non-empty strings, and the spec equality matches the Coq definition.
Both implementations count only a/e/i/o/u (case-insensitive) and add 1 if the last character is y/Y; behavior matches for all strings, including empty.
